threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think people queue for the bus here. It's more of a free-for-all, like a rugby match at peak hours.
I once saw a guy serenading the night bus driver with his guitar. Driver pretended to be unimpressed but was definitely tapping his foot.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a couple arguing over the best Döner kebab while a street musician performed ‘Despacito’ on his ukulele. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a Späti with a full craft beer selection, open all night, right next to a 24-hour Döner joint. Perfect combo!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell locals by their calm when BART shuts down; tourists are the ones standing in the rain with their phones out, bewildered.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy on the 19 Polk humming the mission impossible theme while keeping perfect time with the bumps. Only in SF do ninjas ride Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the 'Gràcia vibe' is all about the bars. They miss the quiet corners with locals tending to their small gardens; it's a whole different world.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can usually tell by their reluctance to try a vermut at the local bar; they stick to 'cue to the next place' like tourists.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep asking for directions to the Overground; locals just know it's all about the bus routes and shortcuts through the back streets.
Last night on the 197, two blokes were arguing over who’s turn it was to offer the bus driver a pint. Never seen that before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy selling homemade hummus from his bike while shouting out the day's politics was when I felt like Neukölln was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a vintage shop that moonlights as a bar. Thrift during the day, cocktails at night—talk about multitasking!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know which taqueria uses the fresh cilantro from the farmer's market and which ones serve the powdered stuff.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing a ukulele on the 19 bus. He turned a crowded commute into a mini concert, even got a reluctant smile from a grumpy commuter.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best tapas are on Carrer del Torrent de l'Olla, but locals know to hit the hidden gems on Carrer de la Perla instead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still search for the nearest Starbucks instead of embracing the local café scene.
